---
title: Kafka UI
description: Multi-cluster Kafka web interface for browsing topics, consumer groups, and messages.
---

import Mermaid from '../../../../components/Mermaid.astro';

**Image:** `provectuslabs/kafka-ui:v0.7.2`  
**Port:** `8080`  
**Data:** `kafka_ui_config` (named volume — persisted cluster configs)  
**Network:** `kafka-ui-net` (external, shared with broker clusters)

## Introduction

[Kafka UI](https://github.com/provectus/kafka-ui) is a free, open-source web interface for Apache Kafka. It supports connecting to multiple clusters simultaneously and provides visual management of topics, consumer groups, brokers, schemas, and messages.

## When to Use

- You want to browse Kafka topics and messages without CLI tools
- You want to monitor consumer group lag visually
- You're managing multiple Kafka clusters and want a single interface
- You need to create or delete topics via a web UI

## Architecture

<Mermaid diagram={`
graph LR
    Browser["Browser"] -->|":8080"| KUI
    subgraph KUI["Kafka UI Container"]
        App["Web Application"]
        Config["kafka_ui_config volume\n(cluster settings)"]
    end
    KUI -->|"kafka-ui-net\nkafka1:29092"| B1["Broker 1"]
    KUI -->|"kafka-ui-net\nkafka2:29093"| B2["Broker 2"]
    KUI -->|"kafka-ui-net\nkafka3:29094"| B3["Broker 3"]
`} />

## Prerequisites

- Docker Engine 20.10+, Docker Compose v2
- `kafka-ui-net` network must exist: `docker network create kafka-ui-net`
- At least one Kafka cluster must be running and reachable via `kafka-ui-net`

## Ports

| Port | Protocol | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `8080` | HTTP | Kafka UI web interface |

Configurable via `KAFKA_UI_PORT`.

:::note[Port conflict]
Port 8080 is also the default for Jenkins. Set `KAFKA_UI_PORT=8081` if running both.
:::

## Configuration

```bash
cd kafka/confluent/kafka-ui
cp .env.example .env
```

| Variable | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `KAFKA_UI_PORT` | `8080` | Web interface port |

Cluster connections are configured via the Kafka UI web interface, not via environment variables.

## Workflow

```bash
# Start
docker compose up -d

# Open UI
open http://localhost:8080

# Add a cluster:
# Settings → Configure new cluster
# Name: my-cluster
# Bootstrap servers: kafka1:29092,kafka2:29093,kafka3:29094
# (for auth clusters: add SASL credentials)

# Stop
docker compose down

# Stop and remove cluster config data
docker compose down -v
```

## Use Cases

**Browse messages in a topic:**
Select cluster → Topics → select topic → Messages tab → set offset and read.

**Monitor consumer lag:**
Select cluster → Consumer Groups → select group → view per-partition lag.

**Create a topic:**
Select cluster → Topics → Add a Topic → set name, partitions, replication factor.

**Multi-cluster management:**
Settings → add multiple clusters (e.g., dev cluster + prod cluster). Switch between them in the top navigation.

## Connecting to the Auth Cluster

When connecting Kafka UI to the `3-node-cluster-with-auth` template:

1. **Settings → Configure new cluster**
2. **Bootstrap servers:** `kafka1:29092,kafka2:29093,kafka3:29094`
3. **Authentication:** SASL/PLAIN
4. **Username:** `admin`
5. **Password:** value of `KAFKA_ADMIN_PASSWORD` from the cluster's `.env`

## Integration

### Nginx Proxy Manager

Uncomment the NPM block in `docker-compose.yml`, restart, and create a proxy host: `kafka-ui.yourdomain.com → kafka-ui:8080`.

## References

- [Kafka UI GitHub](https://github.com/provectus/kafka-ui)
